turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE ROBERTS
turns.text: Justice Thomas has our opinion this morning in case 15-7250, Manrique versus United States.

turns.name: JUSTICE THOMAS
turns.text: This case comes to us on writ of certiorari to the United States Court of Appeals for the Eleventh Circuit. In criminal cases, the court may enter an initial judgment imposing some aspect of a defendant’s sentence and wait until later to enter an amended judgment imposing restitution. That is what happened in this case. Petitioner, Marcelo Manrique was convicted of possessing child pornography. The court entered an initial judgment sentencing him to a term of imprisonment. He filed a notice of appeal from that judgment. Months later, the court entered an amended judgment ordering petitioner to pay restitution to one of the victims. He did not file a second notice of appeal from that and from the amended judgment. Petitioner nonetheless challenged the restitution amount on appeal to the Eleventh Circuit. The government argued that he had forfeited his right to do so by failing to file a second notice of appeal. The Eleventh Circuit agreed. In an opinion filed with the clerk today, we affirm. We hold that a defendant wishing to appeal an order imposing restitution in a deferred restitution case must file a notice of appeal from that order. It is not enough to file a notice of appeal only from the initial judgment. That requirement is at least a mandatory claim processing rule requiring that if the party asserting a violation of the rule timely objects, the Court of Appeals must dismiss the appeal. Here, petitioner failed to file a notice of appeal from the amended judgment. Because the government timely objected to this failure, the Eleventh Circuit correctly dismissed the petitioner’s challenge to the amended judgment. For these reasons and for others set forth in our opinion, we affirm the judgment of the Eleventh Circuit. Justice Ginsburg has filed a dissenting opinion in which Justice Sotomayor joins. Justice Gorsuch took no part in the consideration or decision of this case.
